purpose: Define the Stage-internal cognitive computation kernel that organizes candidate generation, grounded reflection, comparative selection, evolution, meta-review, tool affordances, knowledge, and independent quality gates without turning Route into a small Stage or moving domain truth into OPL.
machine_boundary: This contract defines framework-owned shape, refs, authority boundaries, and required stage-pack declarations. Machine truth remains in source, runtime ledgers, provider receipts, domain-owned manifests, owner receipts, typed blockers, and real workspace/App evidence.
design_summary: Route selects and reconciles owner deltas; Stage attempts execute recoverable work; the cognitive computation kernel is the optional but standard Stage-internal strategy layer that lets stronger AI executors generate, critique, compare, evolve, and learn while OPL keeps only refs, receipts, blockers, and projection boundaries.

kernel_layers.purpose: Parse task intent into stage goal, constraints, desired output attributes, source scope, artifact scope, workspace scope, and safety or human-gate limits.
Generate one or more candidate plans, drafts, hypotheses, artifacts, patches, or route answers using declared prompts, skills, tools, and knowledge refs.
Critique candidates against source refs, private corpus refs, tool evidence, assumptions, correctness, novelty, feasibility, safety, and domain-specific rubrics.
Compare candidates, reduce position bias, cluster similar ideas, keep diversity, and select the next candidate or artifact path for refinement or handoff.
Create improved candidates without mutating accepted artifacts silently; every evolved candidate must retain lineage and pass the same selection and gate rules.
Summarize recurring review patterns, common failure modes, rubric gaps, tool gaps, and stage strategy improvements as versioned refs for future attempts.
Review the selected artifact or closeout packet in a separate attempt, issue an owner receipt, typed blocker, human gate, or route-back decision.

tool_affordance_boundary.policy: OPL standardizes only safety, permission, credential, write-scope, side-effect, and forbidden-authority boundaries. It does not prescribe when, why, or in what order an executor must use tools during a stage attempt.

foundry_agent_implications.mas: Medical research stages should express paper/evidence/reviewer/human-gate cognitive strategies in MAS-owned stage packs while OPL hosts attempts, refs, and projection only.
foundry_agent_implications.mag: Grant stages should express direction, authoring, fundability, export, and submission review strategies in MAG-owned packs while OPL keeps route and attempt boundaries.
foundry_agent_implications.rca: Visual stages should express image-first candidate generation, visual review, export gate, and artifact lineage strategies in RCA-owned packs while OPL hosts the attempt/control plane.
foundry_agent_implications.oma: Agent-building stages should express target-agent candidate, mechanism proposal, independent reviewer, and owner closeout strategies without becoming a second OPL Framework.
